description: Add --refresh-templates and --dry-run flags to hal init that backup and overwrite the 3 core templates (prompt.md, progress.txt, config.yaml) with the latest embedded versions, with detailed per-file status output.

userStories.title: Register --refresh-templates flag on init command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a --refresh-templates flag on hal init so that I can explicitly opt in to overwriting my existing templates with the latest embedded versions.
acceptanceCriteria: initCmd registers a --refresh-templates bool flag defaulting to false
Running hal init without --refresh-templates preserves existing behavior (no template overwrite)
Running hal init --refresh-templates triggers template refresh logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Register --dry-run flag on init command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a --dry-run flag on hal init so that I can preview what --refresh-templates would do without modifying any files.
acceptanceCriteria: initCmd registers a --dry-run bool flag defaulting to false
The --dry-run flag is accessible from the runInit handler
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement core refresh logic with timestamped backup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hal init --refresh-templates to create a timestamped .bak of each existing template before overwriting it so that I can recover my customizations if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: For each of the 3 templates (prompt.md, progress.txt, config.yaml): if the file exists and differs from the embedded version, a backup is created as <filename>.YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.bak in the same directory
After backup, the file is overwritten with the latest embedded content from internal/template
If a template file does not exist, it is created without a backup
If a template file already matches the embedded version, no backup is created and no write occurs
migrateTemplates() still runs after refresh for compatibility
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement dry-run mode for refresh
userStories.description: As a developer, I want --dry-run to preview refresh actions without writing any files so that I can see what would change before committing to it.
acceptanceCriteria: When --refresh-templates --dry-run is passed, no files are written or backed up
Output lines are prefixed with [dry-run] to indicate preview mode
Dry-run output shows the same per-file status as a real run (created/refreshed/unchanged)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add detailed per-file status output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see per-file status lines when refreshing templates so that I know exactly which files were created, refreshed (with backup path), or left unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: Each template file produces one status line: 'created .hal/<file>', 'refreshed .hal/<file> (backup: <file>.YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.bak)', or 'unchanged .hal/<file>'
In dry-run mode, each line is prefixed with [dry-run]
Output is written to io.Writer (not hardcoded to os.Stdout) for testability
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract refreshTemplates into testable function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the refresh logic extracted into a standalone function that accepts io.Writer, halDir, and dryRun parameters so that it can be unit tested without Cobra dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: A function refreshTemplates(halDir string, dryRun bool, w io.Writer) error exists in cmd/init.go
The function handles all 3 templates using template.DefaultFiles() and the backup/write/status logic
runInit delegates to this function when --refresh-templates is set
The function follows the migrateConfigDir pattern of accepting io.Writer for output capture
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add tests for refresh templates functionality
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests covering refresh behavior so that regressions are caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: refreshing when no templates exist creates them without backups
Test: refreshing when templates differ creates timestamped .bak files and overwrites
Test: refreshing when templates already match embedded versions produces 'unchanged' status
Test: dry-run mode produces [dry-run] prefixed output and does not write any files
Tests use t.TempDir() and bytes.Buffer for isolation
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
